---
title: "Chapter 5 - Sannyasa Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">संन्यासयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-04.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-06.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-5-1" data-reference="5.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-1">5.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Kṛṣṇa</span>, you praise renunciation of action and also <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>. Tell me with certainty which of the two is better.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि ।<br>
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-2" data-reference="5.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-2">5.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Renunciation and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">karma-yoga</span> both lead to the highest good, but of the two, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">karma-yoga</span> is superior to renunciation of action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ ।<br>
तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-3" data-reference="5.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-3">5.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He should be known as a perpetual renunciant who neither hates nor desires; free from duality, O mighty-armed one, he is easily freed from bondage.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति ।<br>
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-4" data-reference="5.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-4">5.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Children speak of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sāṅkhya</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> as separate; the wise do not. One who is rightly established in either attains the फल of both.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः ।<br>
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-5" data-reference="5.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-5">5.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">What is attained by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sāṅkhya</span> is also attained by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span>.<br>
He who sees <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sāṅkhya</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">Yoga</span> as one, he sees.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते ।<br>
एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-6" data-reference="5.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-6">5.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But renunciation, O mighty-armed one, is hard to attain without <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>; the sage who is devoted to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> soon attains <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः ।<br>
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-7" data-reference="5.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-7">5.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">United in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, with a purified self, self-controlled, and with the senses conquered, he, whose self has become the self of all beings, is not tainted even while acting.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।<br>
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-8" data-reference="5.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-8">5.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The one who is disciplined and knows the truth should think, “I do nothing at all,” while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, and breathing.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् ।<br>
पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-9" data-reference="5.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-9">5.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes, he should hold that the senses are moving among the objects of the senses.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-10" data-reference="5.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-10">5.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who performs actions, having renounced attachment, and placing them in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is not tainted by water.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः ।<br>
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-11" data-reference="5.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-11">5.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With the body, mind, intellect, and even the senses alone, yogis perform action, having renounced attachment, for the purification of the self.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि ।<br>
योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-12" data-reference="5.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-12">5.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who is disciplined, having renounced the fruit of action, attains steady peace; one who is undisciplined, attached to the fruit through desire, is bound.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् ।<br>
अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-13" data-reference="5.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-13">5.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having renounced all actions in the mind, the embodied one dwells happily, self-controlled, in the city of nine gates, neither acting nor causing action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी ।<br>
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-14" data-reference="5.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-14">5.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The Lord does not create agency, nor actions, nor the connection of action with its fruits for the world; but nature itself proceeds.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः ।<br>
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-15" data-reference="5.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-15">5.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The all-pervading Lord does not take anyone’s sin or merit. Knowledge is veiled by ignorance; therefore beings are deluded.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः ।<br>
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-16" data-reference="5.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-16">5.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But in those whose ignorance has been destroyed by knowledge of the Self, that knowledge reveals the Supreme like the sun.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः ।<br>
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-17" data-reference="5.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-17">5.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those whose understanding is directed toward That, whose self is grounded in That, whose steadiness is in That, and whose final aim is That, go to the state from which there is no return, their impurities having been washed away by knowledge</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः ।<br>
गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-18" data-reference="5.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-18">5.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The wise see with equal vision a learned and humble <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." aria-label="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." title="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." data-glossary-id="brahmin">Brahmin</span>, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and one who eats dogs</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि ।<br>
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-19" data-reference="5.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-19">5.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Here in this world itself, creation is conquered by those whose mind rests in equanimity. For <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> is indeed flawless and even; therefore they are established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः ।<br>
निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-20" data-reference="5.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-20">5.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One should not rejoice on obtaining what is pleasant, nor become agitated on obtaining what is unpleasant; with steady understanding, undeluded, a knower of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, established in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् ।<br>
स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-21" data-reference="5.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-21">5.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With the self unattached to external contacts, one finds the happiness that is in the self.<br>
That one, with the self engaged in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahma-yoga</span>, attains inexhaustible happiness.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् ।<br>
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-22" data-reference="5.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-22">5.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the pleasures born of contact are indeed sources of suffering; they have a beginning and an end, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kaunteya</span>, and the wise do not delight in them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते ।<br>
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-23" data-reference="5.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-23">5.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who is able, while still in this body, to endure the surge born of desire and anger, is a disciplined man; he is a happy man.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् ।<br>
कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-24" data-reference="5.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-24">5.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who is सुख within, रमण within, and whose light is within—having become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, he attains <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">Brahman-nirvana</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः ।<br>
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-25" data-reference="5.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-25">5.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The sages, whose sins have been destroyed, whose doubts are cut away, who are self-controlled, and who delight in the welfare of all beings, attain <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">Brahma-nirvana</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः ।<br>
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-26" data-reference="5.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-26">5.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those ascetics who are free from lust and anger, who have controlled their minds and know the Self, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." aria-label="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." title="nirvana: Extinction, peace, or release; here often linked with Brahman." data-glossary-id="nirvana">Brahman-nirvana</span> lies all around.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् ।<br>
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-27" data-reference="5.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-27">5.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having placed external contacts outside, and the eyes between the eyebrows, and keeping the inhalation and exhalation balanced, moving within the nostrils,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः ।<br>
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-28" data-reference="5.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-28">5.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A sage who has controlled the senses, mind, and intellect, and is devoted to liberation, whose desire, fear, and anger have departed—he is ever मुक्त, indeed.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः ।<br>
विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-5-29" data-reference="5.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-5-29">5.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Knowing Me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great Lord of all the worlds, and the friend of all beings, one attains peace.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।<br>
सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
